name: Nintendo
philosophy: Nintendo's web presence is the brand's signature 'family-friendly fun' translated into a card-rich UI — Nintendo Red (#e60012) as the chromatic anchor on the wordmark and CTAs, generous radii (16–24px) on character cards, and unmistakable IP-driven hero illustrations (Mario, Zelda, Pokemon) carrying the marketing weight. The atmosphere is playful, character-led, and unapologetically colorful where the IP demands it.

design_language.motion_signature: Gentle scroll-triggered reveals on character cards. Hero illustrations may have idle animations (Mario waves, Pikachu blinks). Card hover triggers a soft 200ms scale-up (1.03). The brand is playful but restrained — no chaotic motion.

trademark_signals: Nintendo Red (#e60012) — the brand's signature on the wordmark and primary CTAs
Character-led hero cards — every product page leads with Mario, Zelda's Link, Pikachu, or another IP character
Generous radii (16–24px) on character cards — softness signals friendliness
Switch-shaped product cards (when the product is a game or console) — referencing the console's hardware silhouette
Bright IP-driven secondary colors — Mario red, Zelda gold, Pokemon yellow, Splatoon pink/lime — used only when the IP demands
ai_slop_avoided: Don't replace the character illustrations with generic 3D renders — the IP cast is the brand voice
Don't strip the IP-driven secondary colors when the IP demands them (Mario red on Mario pages)
Don't sharpen card radii to 0–4px — Nintendo's soft radii are the friendliness signal
Don't add edgy or aggressive motion — the brand is family-friendly even on Bowser pages
